ogTitle: Potts Point vs Prairiewood: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Potts Point and Prairiewood.
heading: Potts Point vs Prairiewood — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Potts Point, New South Wales and Prairiewood, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Potts Point has a lower overall climate risk score (44/100) compared to Prairiewood (51/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Potts Point or Prairiewood safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Potts Point has a flood risk score of 48/100, while Prairiewood scores 41/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Potts Point scores 50/100 for bushfire risk. Prairiewood scores 65/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Potts Point or Prairiewood?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Potts Point has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 3.13% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
